# Mirrah — the space between

> Mirrah is what goes between the impulse and the action. A nervous-system regulation companion — body-first, for the 90 seconds between the trigger and the text. “the space between” is its writing on the nervous system, anxious attachment, ADHD, and somatic regulation.

## Articles
- [Why Anxiety Feels Like Excitement (Same Body, Different Story)](https://mirrah.app/blog/anxiety-feels-like-excitement.html): Your pounding heart, tight chest, and racing thoughts are the same signal whether you're anxious or excited. Dr. Lisa Feldman Barrett explains why — and how to change which one wins.
- [Does Naming Your Emotions Actually Work? What the Neuroscience Actually Says](https://mirrah.app/blog/does-naming-your-emotions-work.html): Affect labeling — putting a precise word on a feeling — forces your amygdala to quiet so your language centers can run. Here's the mechanism, the limits, and the right order.
- [Why Loneliness Feels Physical — and Why Scrolling Never Fixes It](https://mirrah.app/blog/why-loneliness-feels-physical.html): Dr. Kay Tye found dedicated "loneliness neurons" in the brain that fire like hunger neurons. Here's why social media can't satisfy them — and what actually does.
- [Why Am I So Emotional for No Reason? Your Bad Mood Might Be a Metabolic Accounting Error](https://mirrah.app/blog/why-am-i-so-emotional-for-no-reason.html): Dr. Lisa Feldman Barrett's body budget explains why your 1 AM spiral might be metabolic, not relational. The emotional flu — and how to tell the difference.
